rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical asymmetric oxidative dimerization of cinnamic acid L-proline amides to furofuran bislactones, precursors of natural lignans (yangambin, sesamin, eudesmin).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (Pt anode) of the electron-rich aryl alkene of the cinnamoyl-proline substrate to an aryl/alkene radical cation, which dimerizes and cyclizes to the furofuran bislactone.

key_intermediates.name: dimeric bis(radical cation)/cyclized cationic intermediate leading to furofuran bislactone

electron_flow: At the Pt anode, the electron-rich arylcinnamate is oxidized by SET to a radical cation; radical-cation coupling/dimerization followed by intramolecular trapping of the carboxylate oxygens onto the resulting cationic centers builds the furofuran bislactone with the proline auxiliary controlling stereochemistry. Aldehyde 3 (ArCHO) is a competing fragmentation product.

probe_reagents.name: L-proline t-butyl ester auxiliary
probe_reagents.observation: Acts as chiral auxiliary; bislactones 2a-d obtained with up to >95% e.e., demonstrating diastereoselective radical-cation coupling.

probe_reagents.name: temperature variation (rt, 0, -20, -40 °C)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower temperature gave higher yield and e.e. (52% yield, 91% e.e. at -40 °C for 1a).

probe_reagents.name: supporting electrolyte screen (Et4NClO4, Et4NOTFA, n-Bu4NPF6, n-Bu4NHSO4, halide salts)
probe_reagents.observation: Halide-containing electrolytes were ineffective; non-coordinating salts gave comparable modest yields with 1c.

probe_reagents.name: protic acid screen (TFA, TfOH, HSO3F, MsOH, HClO4, HBF4·Et2O)
probe_reagents.observation: Only TFA (and trace with TfOH) afforded bislactone, implicating need for a non-nucleophilic Brønsted acid co-solvent to stabilize cationic intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: PbO2/TFA chemical oxidation control
probe_reagents.observation: PbO2 in TFA/CH2Cl2 at -78 °C also produced 2a (64%, >95% e.e.), supporting a SET/radical-cation pathway analogous to the anodic process.

notes: Mechanism is proposed by analogy to Ronlan-type anodic phenolic/styrene oxidative coupling; no TEMPO/BHT trapping, KIE, CV, or DFT reported. Aldehyde 3 (ArCHO) is observed as a C-C cleavage side product, consistent with fragmentation of the radical-cation/dimeric cation manifold. More electron-rich arenes (trimethoxy, methylenedioxy) gave better yields, consistent with easier anodic SET, opposite to a prior racemic chemical synthesis by Yuzikhin.
